message: 🌱 Add kubevela: [Feature] Workflow need support new step type to  batch mission (#783)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kubevela-3460-feature-workflow-need-support-new-step-type-to-batch-apply-a-lot-o",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kubevela: [Feature] Workflow need support new step type to  batch apply a lot of components parallelly or in order",
+    "description": "[Feature] Workflow need support new step type to  batch apply a lot of components parallelly or in order. Requested by 12+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kubevela deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kubevela version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela\nhelm list -n kubevela 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working kubevela installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kubevela configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kubevela configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela\nkubectl get configmap -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevela\n```\n**Request description**\nFor example, I have 6 components in a OAM application，the first and second one need apply before the last four components and wait user to confirm after it apply success."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [Feature] Workflow need support new step type to  batch…",
+        "description": "> After talked with  in community meeting. We found that this is really good scenario for deploy application into clusters in high availability mod. We can use sub-steps to deploy on component to different clusters in parallel.\n> \n> I propose the solution can be like this:\n> \n> ```\n> workflow:\n>  \n```yaml\nworkflow:\n  steps:\n  - type: group\n    name: my-parent-step\n    properties:\n       ...\n    subSteps:\n     - type: deploy\n       name: deploy-comp-1\n       properties:\n           ...\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela\nkubectl get events -n kubevela --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"[Feature] Workflow need support new step type to  batch…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> After talked with  in community meeting. We found that this is really good scenario for deploy application into clusters in high availability mod. We can use sub-steps to deploy on component to different clusters in parallel.\n> \n> I propose the solution can be like this:\n> \n> ```\n> workflow:\n>   steps:\n>   - type: group\n>     name: my-parent-step\n>     properties:\n>        ...",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "workflow:\n  steps:\n  - type: group\n    name: my-parent-step\n    properties:\n       ...\n    subSteps:\n     - type: deploy\n       name: deploy-comp-1\n       properties:\n           ...",
+        "status:\n  workflow:\n    steps:\n    - type: group\n      name: my-parent-step\n      message: ...\n      subSteps:\n      - type:  sub-step1\n        name: deploy-comp-1\n        message: ...",
+        "workflow:\n  steps:\n  - name: my-parent-step\n    subSteps:\n     - type: deploy\n       name: deploy-comp-1\n       properties:\n           ..."
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
